key_metrics.label: FDA Warning (Novo Nordisk)
key_metrics.value: Mar 5 — 3 unreported deaths + systemic PADE failures

key_metrics.label: New Neurological Signal (FAERS)
key_metrics.value: 19 distinct signals incl. allodynia, parosmia, tremor

signals.event_type: Thyroid C-cell tumor risk (medullary thyroid carcinoma)
signals.summary: Rodent studies showed dose-dependent thyroid C-cell tumors. Human epidemiological data from 5+ years of post-marketing surveillance has not confirmed increased MTC risk, but monitoring continues. Black box warning on all GLP-1 RA labels.
signals.action_taken: FDA black box warning. Contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of MTC or MEN2.

signals.summary: Post-marketing reports of acute pancreatitis. Large observational studies suggest a small absolute risk increase (approximately 0.1-0.3% over baseline). Risk appears dose-dependent and higher in patients with prior pancreatitis history.
signals.action_taken: Label warning; recommended monitoring for symptoms and discontinuation if confirmed.

signals.summary: FAERS data showed a higher-than-expected reporting rate for gallbladder events in tirzepatide users compared to semaglutide. Rapid weight loss is a known risk factor for gallstone formation. FDA initiated a formal safety review in January 2026.
signals.action_taken: FDA safety review initiated January 2026. Results expected Q3 2026.

entries.summary: Pharmacovigilance analysis of FDA FAERS database (2005-2024) across 28,953 neurological adverse event reports for six GLP-1 receptor agonists identified 19 distinct neurological signals including dizziness, tremor, dysgeusia, parosmia, allodynia, and hypoglycemic unconsciousness — with a median time-to-onset of 32 days and 45% of events within 30 days.
entries.content: A pharmacovigilance analysis published in Scientific Reports analyzed all neurological adverse event (NAE) reports for six GLP-1 receptor agonists in the FDA FAERS database from 2005 through 2024. Among 28,953 NAE reports, 19 distinct NAE signals were identified across drugs including semaglutide, liraglutide, and tirzepatide. Notable signals include: dizziness, tremor, dysgeusia (taste disturbance), lethargy, taste disorder, presyncope, parosmia (altered smell), allodynia (pain from non-painful stimuli), and hypoglycemic unconsciousness. Time-to-onset analysis revealed a median latency of 32 days (IQR 7-122) for GLP-1 RA-related NAEs, with 45.28% occurring within 30 days of treatment initiation — suggesting these signals emerge early in therapy. This study is notable for being the first comprehensive FAERS analysis of neurological AEs specifically for GLP-1 RAs across the full class. The allodynia and parosmia signals are particularly novel — these had not been prominently featured in prescribing information or previous safety reviews. This study does not establish causality but provides signal-level evidence that may inform future pharmacovigilance monitoring and label updates. The data is especially relevant as millions of new patients in India begin generic semaglutide therapy without the specialist supervision typical of innovator drug prescriptions.

entries.summary: FDA warning letter to Novo Nordisk (March 5, 2026) cited systemic PADE reporting failures: 3 unreported deaths (including 1 suicide), delayed reporting of suicidal ideation, and improper invalidation of stroke cases. FDA said failures affect Novo's 'entire product portfolio.' Novo committed to retrospective review of deviations back to June 2017.
entries.content: The FDA's March 5, 2026 warning letter to Novo Nordisk is the most serious regulatory action against the company since the GLP-1 boom began. An FDA Bioresearch Monitoring Program inspection (January 13 – February 7, 2025) identified systemic failures in Novo's post-marketing adverse drug experience (PADE) reporting: (1) Two patient deaths linked to semaglutide (dated January 30 and December 20, 2024) were not reported to FDA within the required 15-day window; (2) One patient suicide was not reported at all; (3) One suicidal ideation case was submitted late; (4) A stroke case was improperly invalidated because the patient reported it was 'unrelated' — FDA notes the patient's assessment is not grounds for invalidation; (5) A semaglutide suicidal ideation report received December 2024 was not medically reviewed until the FDA inspection began. Root cause identified: Novo's contractor required explicit patient consent before following up on adverse event reports — a consent requirement not mandated by PADE regulations. FDA emphasized violations are 'systemic' and affect 'the entire product portfolio' of semaglutide, liraglutide, nedosiran sodium, and estradiol. Novo Nordisk responded by committing to SOP revisions, in-house intake shift, vendor oversight strengthening, and retrospective review of all closed pharmacovigilance deviations from June 2017 onward. Novo said the warning letter is 'not expected to affect production' or 2026 guidance.

entries.summary: The FDA-approved Wegovy HD (semaglutide 7.2mg) carries a new dysesthesia signal: 22.9% of patients reported altered skin sensation (burning, pain, sensitivity) at the high dose vs 6.0% at 2.4mg and 0.5% on placebo. The FDA is conducting further investigations. GI adverse events also increased to 70.8% vs 61.2% at 2.4mg.
entries.content: Wegovy HD (semaglutide 7.2mg/0.5mL), approved March 19, 2026 under the FDA's Commissioner's National Priority Voucher program just 54 days after filing, carries an emerging safety signal not seen at lower doses: dysesthesia, a form of altered or abnormal skin sensation described as burning, hypersensitivity, tingling, or pain. In the pivotal trial, dysesthesia occurred in 22.9% of patients on semaglutide 7.2mg, compared to 6.0% on the standard 2.4mg dose and just 0.5% on placebo — a dose-dependent signal that increases more than 38-fold versus placebo at the new dose. The FDA noted these reactions generally resolved on their own or with dose reduction, but stated it is 'currently conducting further investigations regarding this drug reaction.' Other adverse event increases at the 7.2mg dose include: total GI adverse events 70.8% (vs 61.2% at 2.4mg), nausea 47.9% vs 42.1%, vomiting 23.4% vs 19.0%, and serious adverse events 6.8% vs 5.5%. The approval was granted under expedited review for continued weight loss maintenance in patients who plateaued at 2.4mg, addressing a key unmet need but with heightened safety monitoring requirements. This safety profile will be important context for Elevate Now and similar programs deciding whether to adopt the higher dose.

entries.summary: The FDA's ongoing safety review of tirzepatide-associated gallbladder events received attention after a brief NEJM communication reported gallbladder event rates of 2.8% in the SURMOUNT-4 extension study.
entries.content: The FDA's ongoing safety review of tirzepatide-associated gallbladder events received attention after a brief communication in NEJM reported gallbladder event rates of 2.8% in the SURMOUNT-4 extension study. Investigators noted that rapid weight loss itself is a well-established gallstone risk factor, complicating causal assessment.

A 2026 assessment published in Diabetes, Obesity and Metabolism evaluated thyroid cancer risk associated with GLP-1 receptor agonist use in a large propensity-matched cohort using the TriNetX electronic health record database. The study found no increased thyroid cancer risk compared with active comparators including insulin, metformin, or SGLT2 inhibitors. The FDA's boxed warning, based on rodent studies showing dose-dependent C-cell tumors, remains in place as a precaution, though five-plus years of post-marketing surveillance in humans has not confirmed an increased risk of medullary thyroid carcinoma. Medullary thyroid cancer comprises only about 3% of all thyroid cancers, and the common types (papillary, follicular) are not implicated.
entries.summary: Large cohort study finds no increased thyroid cancer risk with GLP-1 RAs despite FDA boxed warning

entries.content: A systematic review and meta-analysis published in September 2025 in Endocrinology, Diabetes & Metabolism analyzed 62 randomized controlled trials covering 66,232 patients with a mean follow-up of 43.5 weeks. The pooled analysis showed a small but statistically significant increase in pancreatitis risk (RR 1.44, 95% CI 1.09-1.89), but this signal lost significance when stratified by background medication use. A separate semaglutide-specific meta-analysis found no increased risk of acute pancreatitis (OR 0.7, 95% CI 0.5-1.2) regardless of dose or formulation. Liraglutide showed the highest pharmacovigilance signal among GLP-1 RAs, while tirzepatide demonstrated the lowest.
entries.summary: Meta-analysis of 62 RCTs finds small pancreatitis signal with GLP-1 RAs, not significant after stratification

entries.source_name: Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism (Oxford Academic)
entries.content: In January 2025, the FDA updated Ozempic's label to state the drug is 'not recommended in patients with severe gastroparesis,' following growing reports of delayed gastric emptying and stomach paralysis. One study found that 75% of participants with normal gastric emptying before GLP-1 RA treatment developed delayed gastric emptying, and 30% with pre-existing diabetic gastroparesis experienced worsening. As of March 2026, 3,363 lawsuits have been filed in multidistrict litigation alleging injuries including severe gastroparesis and intestinal obstruction. The American Society of Anesthesiologists now recommends holding GLP-1 agonists one week before elective surgery due to aspiration risk.
entries.summary: FDA updates Ozempic label for gastroparesis risk amid growing litigation

entries.content: The FDA requested that drug manufacturers remove information about suicidal ideation and behavior risk from GLP-1 RA labeling for Saxenda (liraglutide), Wegovy (semaglutide), and Zepbound (tirzepatide), following a comprehensive review that found no increased risk. The EMA had reached the same conclusion in April 2024 after reviewing 150 case reports. A Lancet eClinicalMedicine study using propensity-matched data found semaglutide was actually associated with lower risk of suicidal ideation (HR 0.27) compared to other agents. However, pharmacovigilance data showed that patients co-prescribed antidepressants or benzodiazepines had substantially higher reporting odds ratios, suggesting those with pre-existing psychiatric conditions may warrant closer monitoring.
entries.summary: FDA requests removal of suicidal ideation warning from GLP-1 RA labels after comprehensive review

entries.content: A 2024 report published in Clinical Kidney Journal documented cases of semaglutide-associated acute interstitial nephritis and podocytopathy, with patients experiencing rapid worsening of kidney function and increased proteinuria after starting semaglutide. FAERS data from 2010-2022 identified 2,375 kidney-related adverse events associated with GLP-1 RAs, with acute kidney injury comprising 58.65% of reports. The SUSTAIN clinical trials also reported higher AKI rates with semaglutide than comparators (8 events vs 1 with dulaglutide). However, the large FLOW trial published in NEJM demonstrated renoprotective benefits of semaglutide in T2DM patients with CKD, suggesting the risk-benefit equation may vary significantly by patient population and kidney function at baseline.
entries.summary: Case series of semaglutide-associated kidney injury raises monitoring concerns

entries.content: A 2025 narrative review in Diabetes, Obesity and Metabolism synthesizing real-world evidence from observational studies of liraglutide, semaglutide, and tirzepatide found that gastrointestinal issues remain the most common adverse effects but are generally manageable and decrease over time. The review found no strong real-world evidence of severe safety signals beyond what was seen in clinical trials. However, real-world discontinuation rates remain high at 20-50% within the first year, with many patients using lower doses than evaluated in trials. A recent pharmacy claims analysis showed improvement, with 63% of patients starting Wegovy or Zepbound in early 2024 still on therapy at one year, up from 40% among 2023 initiators.
entries.summary: Large real-world evidence review confirms manageable GLP-1 safety profile but high discontinuation rates
